text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data, which includes examples with correct answers. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules or patterns. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, it might predict whether an email is spam or not. The accuracy of the model measures how often its predictions are correct. A more accurate model is better at its task.
